_description: Template for your own golden self-test cases. Copy to golden-cases.json and add your validated closed PRs. Commander runs workers against these in dryrun mode and compares output. A regression means a commander prompt change made worker output meaningfully worse.
_how_to_add: After a manual Stage-0 validation of a closed PR in one of your repos, capture: repo, pr_num, parent_commit (pre-fix), key assertions (what the worker MUST produce), and minimum quality signals (tests added? no source regressions? build passes?).

_requires_legend.requires_docker: Skip the case if `docker info` fails (no docker daemon reachable). Used by cases that spin up localstack / test containers.
_requires_legend.requires_linear_mcp: Skip the case if the Linear MCP server isn't installed.
_requires_legend.requires_gh_auth: Skip the case if `gh auth status` fails.

cases.ticket_body_summary: Short description of what the ticket asked for, as you'd write it if re-opening the issue today

cases._comment: STUB for the retro command (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-retro-workflow.md). Populate `fixture_logs_path`, `fixture_citations_path`, and `expected_sections` with real values once you have a week of real logs. Until then, this stub documents the shape so the next operator knows what a retro golden case looks like. Runs against the commander itself (no worker subagent) — retro is an inline commander procedure, not a spawned worker.
cases.ticket_body_summary: Run `retro` against a fixed snapshot of logs/ + state/memory-citations.json + memory/escalation-faq.md and verify the output matches the template structure in CLAUDE.md (Weekly retro section).

cases._comment: Commander-side fixture — not a PR regression. Validates scheduled autopickup's tick procedure against simulated queue/PAUSE/rate-limit states. Verifies quiet-by-default, yield-to-foreground, and 2-strike rate-limit auto-disable. See docs/specs/2026-04-16-scheduled-autopickup.md and CLAUDE.md 'Scheduled autopickup'.
cases.ticket_body_summary: Operator runs `autopickup every 5 min`. Simulate 3 consecutive ticks under different fixture conditions and verify commander-side behavior.

ticks._comment: Healthy queue; two ready tickets, no active workers, no pause, no rate-limit.
Rate-limit preflight fails — no spawn, increment consecutive_rate_limit_hits, stay quiet (this is the FIRST strike, not yet at the 2-strike threshold).
Second consecutive rate-limit preflight failure — hit the 2-strike auto-disable. enabled flips to false, ONE chat message surfaces, /loop exits.

yield_behavior_check._comment: Separate assertion: if operator types a message during a tick window, the tick must NOT race the foreground turn. Commander handles the operator first, resumes on next scheduled tick.
yield_behavior_check.expected: Tick #2 is deferred; 'status' gets a normal response; tick runs at next interval boundary.

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the default-on autopickup behavior (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-autopickup-default-on.md, ticket #17). Verifies that a fresh setup.sh seeds state/autopickup.json with auto_enable_on_session_start=true, and that the ./hydra launcher's --no-autopickup flag exports HYDRA_NO_AUTOPICKUP=1. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree. setup.sh is run non-interactively with an empty stdin so the external-memory-dir prompt accepts the default. setup.sh hard-fails its prereq check if `claude` is not on PATH, so this case is marked requires_claude_cli — it SKIPs on CI runners where the Claude Code CLI isn't installed.

steps.name: fresh setup.sh seeds state/autopickup.json with auto_enable_on_session_start=true
steps.cmd: rm -f state/autopickup.json && bash setup.sh <<<'' >/dev/null && jq -r .auto_enable_on_session_start state/autopickup.json

steps.name: fresh setup.sh seeds enabled=false (session-start logic flips it, not setup)
HYDRA_NO_AUTOPICKUP env var is visible to the launcher's env

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-doctor.sh. Exercises the Scripts category in isolation — deterministic, doesn't depend on external state, so it's safe to run in any Hydra worktree. Also asserts --help prints usage. Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/hydra-doctor.sh. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-hydra-doctor.md (ticket #36).

Script-only self-test: exercises scripts/linear-poll.sh against the mock Linear MCP response under self-test/fixtures/linear/. Runs locally; no Linear MCP or credentials required. Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/linear-poll.sh, state/linear.example.json, or docs/linear-tool-contract.md. The fixture pins the assumed Linear MCP response shape — if an operator validates that shape against their real Linear MCP install and it differs, the fixture AND the tool contract doc must be updated together (same PR).

steps.name: linear-poll.sh --fixture parses mock response
steps.expected_stdout_contains: OK: linear-poll.sh --fixture parses mock response correctly

cases._description: Script-only self-test: exercises scripts/parse-citations.sh, scripts/validate-learning-entry.sh, and scripts/validate-citations.sh against fixtures under self-test/fixtures/memory-validator/. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree. Commander runs this before committing changes to any of those scripts or the memory schema.

steps.name: parse-citations emits citations delta from fixture report
validate-learning-entry fails on fixture missing frontmatter
validate-citations passes when all quotes are live

cases._description: Self-tests the self-test runner. Invokes ./self-test/run.sh against fake-golden-cases.json fixtures (self-test/fixtures/self-test-runner/) — one deliberately passing case, one deliberately failing — and asserts exit codes + error-message shape. Runs before committing changes to self-test/run.sh itself. See docs/specs/2026-04-16-self-test-runner.md.

steps.name: runner failure output names the failing step
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 ./self-test/run.sh --cases-file self-test/fixtures/self-test-runner/fake-golden-cases.json --case fake-fail 2>&1 | grep -F 'second step intentionally fails'

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/release.sh (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-release-process.md, ticket #28). Stages a minimal mirror tree in a tmpdir with fixture VERSION + CHANGELOG.md, invokes release.sh against it, verifies --dry-run does not mutate files and that downgrade / equal-version / non-semver all exit non-zero. Runs before committing changes to scripts/release.sh, VERSION, or CHANGELOG.md.

Script-only self-test for the hydra-operator Main Agent skill (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-hydra-operator-skill.md, ticket #53). Verifies that skills/hydra-operator/SKILL.md exists with the required YAML frontmatter fields (name, description, mcpServers) that Claude Code's skill loader needs, that the install README is present and contains the operator-facing `cp -r` install command, and that copying the skill directory into a fake user-scoped skills dir produces a readable SKILL.md. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree. Commander runs this before committing changes to skills/hydra-operator/.

steps.name: install-target SKILL.md exists at the expected path
install README exists and documents the cp -r install command
install smoke — copying into a fake skills dir yields a readable SKILL.md
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cp -r skills/hydra-operator "$tmp/" && ls "$tmp/hydra-operator/SKILL.md" && echo OK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/state-get.sh + scripts/state-put.sh against a localstack-backed DynamoDB (Phase 2 adapter — see docs/specs/2026-04-16-s3-knowledge-store.md and ticket #13). Spins up localstack via docker compose, creates the bucket + commander-* tables, puts a JSON blob via state-put.sh (dynamodb backend, localstack endpoint), reads it back via state-get.sh, compares bit-for-bit, tears down. Marked requires_docker: skipped on machines where `docker info` fails.

steps.name: local-mode smoke — backend unset defaults to local and roundtrips a JSON blob
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && echo '{"workers":[],"last_updated":null}' > "$tmp/active.json" && scripts/state-get.sh --key active --state-dir "$tmp" && echo '{"workers":[{"id":"w1"}],"last_updated":"2026-04-16T00:00:00Z"}' | scripts/state-put.sh --key active --state-dir "$tmp" && scripts/state-get.sh --key active --state-dir "$tmp"

steps.name: memory-mount.sh --dry-run prints the mount-s3 command when env is set
localstack e2e roundtrip (put via dynamodb backend, get, compare)

cases._description: Exercises worker-conflict-resolver on two PRs that both added rows to the same markdown commands table in CLAUDE.md. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-worker-conflict-resolver.md (ticket #7). SKIPped in the current runner — needs a worker-subagent-kind executor (same path that ticket #15 blocks on). Fixture under self-test/fixtures/conflict-resolver/: base-commands-table.md (starting state, 5 rows), pr-a-diff.patch (adds 'retro'), pr-b-diff.patch (adds 'autopickup'), expected-resolution.md (both rows, no dupes), semantic-conflict-example.md (documented STOP-and-escalate case, for contrast).
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-conflict-resolver spawn against live PRs; implement when worker-subagent SKIP-path executor lands
cases.ticket_body_summary: Two open PRs both add a new row to the same markdown commands table in CLAUDE.md. Commander invokes worker-conflict-resolver to merge them deterministically into one superseding PR.

assertions.expected_artifact: worker opens a draft PR titled 'Merge #A + #B into main' that supersedes both source PRs; resolution contains both added table rows with no duplicates; source PRs are labeled commander-superseded and get a comment linking the new PR

cases._description: Exercises worker-test-discovery end-to-end against a minimal fixture repo (self-test/fixtures/test-discovery/fixture-repo/) that has no TESTING.md, no CLAUDE.md, and no README testing section. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-exercise-test-discovery.md (ticket #32). Today the runner SKIPs this case — it needs the same worker-implementation SKIP-path executor that ticket #15 is about to land. The follow-up ticket (#15-follow-up) is to switch this case from SKIP to live execution once that executor is merged.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-test-discovery spawn; implement when worker-implementation SKIP path lands
cases.ticket_body_summary: Worker-implementation has asked 'test procedure unclear' twice on this repo. Commander auto-dispatched worker-test-discovery to document how to run + verify the service.

assertions.expected_artifact: worker opens a PR adding TESTING.md to the fixture repo with the docker compose up + curl/redis-cli localhost:6379 verify steps

cases._description: Verifies that a Commander-spawned worker subagent correctly inherits the strict permission denylist from .claude/settings.local.json. Executes self-test/fixtures/permission-probe/probe-script.sh inside a worker subagent, captures JSONL output, diffs against self-test/fixtures/permission-probe/expected-outcomes.json. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-permission-audit.md. Audit doc: docs/security-audit-2026-04-17-permission-inheritance.md. Ticket: #31. Today the runner SKIPs this case — same worker-subagent execution path that test-discovery-dormant-to-active needs (ticket #15 follow-up). Until then, the operator runs the probe manually per the audit doc's 'Manual operator run' section.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-implementation SKIP-path executor; when that lands, this case becomes live and gives the operator a per-commit receipt on permission-inheritance
cases.ticket_body_summary: Permission inheritance probe. Spawn a worker subagent whose only job is to `bash self-test/fixtures/permission-probe/probe-script.sh --worktree-root "$PWD" --output /tmp/permission-probe-observed.jsonl`, then emit the captured JSONL back to Commander. Commander diffs observed vs expected-outcomes.json; any mismatch is a finding.
